RE_ASTRAL_PARTY_MOD_EVENT_ASTRAL_RELIC_S:
Before you even reach the stall, you spot a single coin on the ground.

The shopkeeper glances over and shrugs.

"Pick it up first. Hard to shop with less than one gold."

A makeshift stall blocks the roadside, its shelves lined with polished token packs.

The shopkeeper barely lifts a hand, as if they already expected you.

"Buy fast. No haggling here. Just show me how much gold you're willing to spend."

You slap the gold onto the counter and trade it for a [◈ Token Pack].

The shopkeeper nods. "You know what you're buying. Bring more gold next time if you want bigger odds."

You buy the most expensive [◈◈◈ Token Pack] without hesitation.

The shopkeeper smiles. "Now that's someone willing to spend properly."

You take the heavier [◈◈ Token Pack].

The shopkeeper weighs the coins in hand. "That one was worth the price."

The shopkeeper stares at your single coin for a moment, then shoves [Initial Point] into your hands.

"A bargain's a bargain. Take it and move along."
RE_ASTRAL_PARTY_MOD_EVENT_VIAL_EPISODE_S:
Before you reach the shelf, you notice a lone coin rolling on the floor.

No one stops you from taking it.

A row of sealed vials sways in front of you. Inside each one, a compressed event fragment keeps flashing on repeat.

"Expensive ones are stable. Cheap ones are exciting. Pick your poison."
Pay [gold]1[/gold] Gold. Obtain [Vial: Anomaly Event]. Automatically plays [card]Transfer Request[/card] when used.

Pay [gold]15[/gold] Gold. Obtain [Vial: Neutral Event]. When used, randomly draw 3 from [card]Crowded Passage[/card], [card]Equality[/card], [card]Player Representative[/card], and [card]Thunder Approaches[/card], then choose 1 to play.

Pay [gold]37[/gold] Gold. Obtain [Vial: Good Event]. When used, randomly draw 3 from [card]Fight, Fun![/card], [card]Gift from Sky[/card], [card]Red Heat Warning[/card], and [card]Sprint[/card], then choose 1 to play.
Pay [gold]89[/gold] Gold. Obtain [Vial: Deus Ex Machina]. Automatically plays [card]Deus Ex Machina[/card] when used.
You pick up the cheapest vial. Static crackles inside like it might burst open at any moment.
This one feels balanced enough to be suspiciously ordinary.
A warm glow leaks from the glass. At least this one does not look immediately dangerous.
The most expensive vial is heavier than it looks, as if someone compressed a whole miracle into the bottle.
RE_ASTRAL_PARTY_MOD_EVENT_DREAM_SPARKLIN:
Deep in the dream, two wells stand side by side.

One is clear enough to wash away excess refinement. The other shines with drifting light, as if it could push unfinished things one step further.

No one guards them. The invitation feels almost clinical.
Choose 1 unupgraded upgradable card to upgrade, then end the event.
Choose any number of upgraded cards and revert them to their unupgraded state, then proceed to [Sparkling Well].

The afterglow from purification does not fade. Instead, it slips through the cracks and gathers in the other well.

Points of light drift across the surface, waiting for you to assign them somewhere new.
From all unupgraded upgradable cards, choose exactly the required number to upgrade.

A faint ripple spreads across the well, and the chosen card's lines are redrawn with sharper intent.

You do not linger. The dream closes around that single change.

The purified light circles the rim for a while, then slowly sinks back into stillness.

Both wells quiet down, and you leave them behind.

The cards you rekindle cast brief reflections over the water before the light settles fully into the paper.

The dream offers nothing more.

The sparkling well searches your deck for something that can still be refined, but finds nothing.

This meeting ends there.

The purifying well reflects your deck in silence, yet not a single card bears a refinement it can wash away.

That path closes immediately.

After purification, the sparkling well offers more light than your remaining cards can properly receive.

The surface dims on its own, refusing an incomplete exchange.
Choose exactly the required number of cards to upgrade
